title: Everyday Slang
description: The casual, current expressions young Filipinos actually use with friends, online, and out in the barkada every day.
situation: Sounding natural and relaxed - greetings, reactions, hype, and filler you would hear hanging out with friends.

phrases.tip: The chopped, super-casual version of kumusta. Texting and barkada default; drop the po for friends.
Classic slang for cool. It's the word tigas (tough/hard) flipped around. Ang astig! = how cool!
Praise for something or someone impressive and confident. Ang angas mo! = you're so cool.
Idol spelled backwards. Playful praise for someone you admire, like calling them a legend.
Syllable-swapped malupit (savage/intense). Part of the same wordplay craze as lodi and werpa.
The word power flipped into syllables. A shout of support and good vibes, like sending strength.
May chika ako! = I've got tea! Magchika tayo = let's catch up. Light and friendly, never heavy.
Just the letter G (from game). The all-purpose yes when someone proposes a plan. G na G = totally down.

phrases.english: Lucky them, I wish I had that
phrases.tip: Half-playful, half-envious reaction to someone's blessing, like a couple posting. Sana = I hope; all = everyone.
Tagged onto the end of a joke or a tease so no one takes it seriously. Also said as charR or char.
Borrowed from English but everywhere among young Filipinos for a party or event that's great. Ang lit!

phrases.english: Yes! nailed it / there it is
phrases.tip: A hyped exclamation of approval when something lands perfectly. Pak is the sound of a hit; ganern = like that.
Chill and relax mashed together, with lang (just) tacked on. Telling someone to take it easy.
From the English word, but Filipinos use it for being totally drained. Haggard ako sa work = work wrecked me.
The everyday call to head out or get moving. Tara na! = let's go already! Tara, kain = come on, let's eat.

phrases.english: What a waste / aw, too bad
phrases.tip: A light exclamation of regret when something good is missed or lost. Widely used by everyone, all ages.
